initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['delegate']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Break the problem into two mathematical subproblems for other agents.

Subproblem 1: Determine if k(n)=1 is possible by checking if 2023 is a power of 2. Use its prime factorization to justify.
Subproblem 2: Determine if k(n)=2 is possible by checking if 2023 divides 2^a + 2^b for some integers a, b. If not, investigate if k(n)=3 is possible.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "delegate",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "helpers": [
    {
      "label": "helper_1",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the first helper"
    },
    {
      "label": "helper_2",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the second helper"
    }
  ],
  "synthesis_brief": "how the parent should combine the child results"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Break the problem into two mathematical subproblems for other agents.

Subproblem 1: Determine if k(n)=1 is possible by checking if 2023 is a power of 2. Use its prime factorization to justify.
Subproblem 2: Determine if k(n)=2 is possible by checking if 2023 divides 2^a + 2^b for some integers a, b. If not, investigate if k(n)=3 is possible.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "delegate",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "helpers": [
    {
      "label": "helper_1",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the first helper"
    },
    {
      "label": "helper_2",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the second helper"
    }
  ],
  "synthesis_brief": "how the parent should combine the child results"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B6-binary-representation-minimization
Title: B6. Binary Representation Minimization

Question:
For each n, let k(n) be the number of ones in the binary representation of 2023*n.
Find the minimum k(n).

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
final_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 2
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['execute_locally']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}

Synthesis brief from the earlier child-creation step:
The parent agent will take the results from helper_1 and helper_2 to determine the minimum k(n). If helper_1 finds k(n)=1 is possible, the answer is 1. If helper_1 says no and helper_2 finds k(n)=2 is possible, the answer is 2. If both are impossible, the agent will evaluate the findings for k(n)=3 to determine the final minimum value.

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: No\nJUSTIFICATION: 2023 is not a power of 2 because it is divisible by 7 and 17."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_02",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: k(n)=2 is not possible, but k(n)=3 is possible.\nJUSTIFICATION: 2^a + 2^b mod 2023 cannot be 0 because 2023 is not a divisor of any 2^x(2^y+1), while k(n)=3 is possible via the existence of a subset sum in the cyclic group."
  }
]

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B6-binary-representation-minimization
Title: B6. Binary Representation Minimization

Question:

JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
